title: IPv4 Addressing, CIDR, VLSM, and Subnet Design · Incident Repair Bay
summary: Create an address plan for departments, infrastructure, guests, point-to-point links, growth, and route summarization. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a validated IPv4 subnet allocation workbook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Convert IPv4 octets and masks between binary and dotted decimal and explain contiguous prefix bits.
Identify RFC1918, APIPA, loopback, unspecified, limited broadcast, multicast, and public addressing behavior.
Calculate network address, broadcast address, usable host range, prefix length, and address count for CIDR blocks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Net Ipv4 Address Plan evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Net Ipv4 Address Plan assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Document scopes, gateways, reservations, exclusions, VLANs, DHCP pools, static infrastructure, and IPAM ownership. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network ipv4 subnetting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Ipv4 Address Plan, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a validated IPv4 subnet allocation workbook while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: An address spreadsheet is complete even when it omits VLAN and DHCP relationships. This fails the Net Ipv4 Address Plan contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Internet Protocol Version 6 Specification (RFC 8200) — Controls IPv6 packet, addressing, extension, and transition explanations at foundational depth.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document scopes, gateways, reservations, exclusions, VLANs, DHCP pools, static infrastructure, and IPAM ownership.

hints: Name the Net Ipv4 Address Plan precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed debug case derived from this module: network ipv4 subnetting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: An address spreadsheet is complete even when it omits VLAN and DHCP relationships.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-ipv4-address-plan-1glxyb" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Ipv4 Address Plan into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: net-ipv4-address-plan-1glxyb
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable probable cause]
# command: choose one safe read-only simulator command
# observation: [replace with the result that supports or rejects the hypothesis]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Ipv4 Address Plan applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Document scopes, gateways, reservations, exclusions, VLANs, DHCP pools, static infrastructure, and IPAM ownership.
